message:
chore(beads): Close bd-iuvb.16 (Navigation IA) as completed

Mark bd-iuvb.16 as closed with reason documenting deliverables:
- Palette JSONL logging implemented
- Snapshot tests for empty, filtered, favorites states
- Conformal tie-breaks ranking test
- Help overlay category legend
- E2E test step 17 for palette flow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -847,7 +847,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.13","title":"Demo: Live Markdown Editor (Split View)","description":"Background
- Markdown rendering exists, but live editing + preview is more compelling.

Goals
- Split-pane editor + preview with live updates.
- Highlight wrapping, code fences, and links.
- Provide search within the editor with results highlighted.

Menu/IA Fit
- Category: Text.
- Short label: "Markdown".
- Registry tags: "editor", "preview", "markdown".

Implementation Notes
- Use ftui-text rope/editor and ftui-extras markdown renderer.
- Deterministic seed and sample doc for snapshots.
- Provide a "diff mode" to compare raw vs rendered width.

Acceptance Criteria
- Editing updates preview in real time without flicker.
- Search highlights and jumps work reliably.
- Preview correctly renders lists, code blocks, links.
- Split pane remains stable under resize.
- Screen registered in Screen Registry (category, label, tags, blurb).

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for editor search and cursor operations.
- Snapshot tests for sample doc at two sizes.
- E2E PTY run (extend `scripts/e2e_demo_showcase.sh`) with scripted edits and JSONL logs:
  - run_id, edit_seq, search_term, result_count, outcome","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlueCove","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:51:26.030601617Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T05:06:17.845632835Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.13","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:51:26.030601617Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.14","title":"Demo: Hyperlink Playground (OSC-8 + Hit Regions)","description":"Background
- OSC-8 hyperlinks and hit regions are powerful but under-explained.

Goals
- Display clickable links with hover/focus states.
- Show link registry IDs and OSC-8 sequences for education.
- Demonstrate keyboard navigation among links.

Menu/IA Fit
- Category: Interaction.
- Short label: "Links".
- Registry tags: "osc8", "hit", "link".

Implementation Notes
- Use LinkRegistry and HitGrid integration.
- Provide deterministic link list with sample URLs.
- Add a "raw sequence" panel for educational display.

Acceptance Criteria
- Links render with distinct styling and focus ring.
- Hover and keyboard focus highlight are visible.
- Optional panel shows link registry ID mapping and sequences.
- Screen registered in Screen Registry (category, label, tags, blurb).

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for link registry behavior in demo state.
- Snapshot tests for normal, hover, and focus states.
- E2E PTY run (extend `scripts/e2e_demo_showcase.sh`) with scripted focus movement and JSONL logs:
  - run_id, link_id, focus_idx, action, outcome","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:51:31.797391729Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T05:04:42.818620204Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.14","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:51:31.797391729Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.15","title":"Demo: Performance Challenge Mode (Degradation Tiers)","description":"Background
- Degradation tiers and budgets are critical but not demonstrated interactively.

Goals
- Provide a "stress" mode that intentionally overwhelms rendering.
- Show real-time degradation tier transitions and recovery.
- Display budget metrics and decision evidence.

Menu/IA Fit
- Category: Systems.
- Short label: "Perf".
- Registry tags: "budget", "tier", "latency".

Implementation Notes
- Reuse performance HUD metrics and render budget info.
- Add deterministic stress scenarios (fixed seed, fixed workload).
- Include a "cool down" button to show recovery.

Acceptance Criteria
- Stress toggle reliably triggers degradation and recovery.
- UI clearly shows current tier and decision rationale.
- Runs without stalling input handling.
- Evidence entries are recorded for each tier change.
- Screen registered in Screen Registry (category, label, tags, blurb).

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for stress scenario generator determinism.
- Snapshot tests for each degradation tier.
- E2E PTY run (extend `scripts/e2e_demo_showcase.sh`) with JSONL logs:
  - run_id, tier_from, tier_to, frame_time_ms, decision, outcome","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:51:39.834624358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T03:06:37.757497025Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.15","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:52:13.446170028Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.15","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:51:39.834624358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-iuvb.16","title":"Demo: Navigation IA (Screen Registry + Palette + Category Tabs)","description":"Background
- With 30+ screens, navigation can become overwhelming without a strong IA.
- A dedicated registry and palette make the demo feel intentional and fast.

Goals
- Implement Screen Registry (id/title/short_label/category/order/palette_tags/blurb/hotkey).
- Implement category tabs + category-aware navigation.
- Implement Screen Palette (Ctrl+K) with search, filters, favorites, and ranking.

Menu/IA Fit
- Category: Tour/Core/Visuals/Interaction/Text/Systems displayed with color pills.
- Tab bar shows short_label + category color.
- Shift+Left/Right jumps categories; Left/Right moves within category.

Implementation Notes
- Registry is the single source of truth for screen list and ordering.
- Palette ranking: exact > prefix > word-start > substring (stable tie-breakers).
- Favorites are persisted in memory for session; optional future persistence.

Acceptance Criteria
- Registry drives tab bar, palette, and help overlay (no duplicated lists).
- Palette search is fast and deterministic (fixed ordering for equal scores).
- Category navigation works from any screen.
- All screens are registered with category, label, tags, and blurb.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for registry ordering and palette ranking.
- Snapshot tests for palette (empty, filtered, favorites) at 80x24 and 120x40.
- E2E PTY run (extend `scripts/e2e_demo_showcase.sh`) with JSONL logs:
  - run_id, action, query, selected_screen, category, outcome","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacOwl","created_at":"2026-02-04T03:04:32.705019824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T06:30:38.075846900Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.16","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T03:04:32.705019824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-iuvb.16","title":"Demo: Navigation IA (Screen Registry + Palette + Category Tabs)","description":"Background
- With 30+ screens, navigation can become overwhelming without a strong IA.
- A dedicated registry and palette make the demo feel intentional and fast.

Goals
- Implement Screen Registry (id/title/short_label/category/order/palette_tags/blurb/hotkey).
- Implement category tabs + category-aware navigation.
- Implement Screen Palette (Ctrl+K) with search, filters, favorites, and ranking.

Menu/IA Fit
- Category: Tour/Core/Visuals/Interaction/Text/Systems displayed with color pills.
- Tab bar shows short_label + category color.
- Shift+Left/Right jumps categories; Left/Right moves within category.

Implementation Notes
- Registry is the single source of truth for screen list and ordering.
- Palette ranking: exact > prefix > word-start > substring (stable tie-breakers).
- Favorites are persisted in memory for session; optional future persistence.

Acceptance Criteria
- Registry drives tab bar, palette, and help overlay (no duplicated lists).
- Palette search is fast and deterministic (fixed ordering for equal scores).
- Category navigation works from any screen.
- All screens are registered with category, label, tags, and blurb.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for registry ordering and palette ranking.
- Snapshot tests for palette (empty, filtered, favorites) at 80x24 and 120x40.
- E2E PTY run (extend `scripts/e2e_demo_showcase.sh`) with JSONL logs:
  - run_id, action, query, selected_screen, category, outcome","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacOwl","created_at":"2026-02-04T03:04:32.705019824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T07:39:45.103372323Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T07:39:45.103354269Z","close_reason":"Completed: palette logging, snapshots, ranking test, help legend, E2E step","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.16","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T03:04:32.705019824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.2","title":"Demo: Determinism Lab (Checksum Equivalence)","description":"Background
- Correctness and determinism are core differentiators, but currently implicit.
- A dedicated lab can make the proof visible and visceral.

Goals
- Render identical scenes through multiple diff strategies and show checksum equivalence.
- Display per-frame change counts and mismatch warnings.
- Allow toggling strategy (Full, DirtyRows, Redraw) and seed.

Menu/IA Fit
- Category: Systems.
- Short label: "Determinism".
- Indexed in Screen Registry with palette tags: "checksum", "diff", "proof".

Implementation Notes
- Use TerminalModel checksum or render-trace checksum logic for validation.
- Visualize mismatch as red banner with first differing coordinate and delta count.
- Include a mini timeline of checksum history for the last N frames.
- Deterministic with fixed seed and frozen time.

Acceptance Criteria
- Checksums match across strategies for identical inputs.
- Mismatch path shows first differing coordinate and delta count.
- User can export a verification report to JSONL.
- UI remains responsive under rapid toggling.
- Screen registered in Screen Registry (category, label, tags, blurb).

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for checksum equivalence across strategies and seeds.
- Snapshot tests for match and mismatch UI states.
- E2E PTY run (extend `scripts/e2e_demo_showcase.sh`):
  - run_id, strategy, seed, size, checksum, mismatch_coord, outcome","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:50:08.514276659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T06:25:45.318967806Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T06:25:45.318949251Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.4.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:51:52.062308884Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.4.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:51:55.594243894Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:50:08.514276659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.3","title":"Demo: Inline Mode Story (Scrollback + Chrome)","description":"Background
- Inline mode is a headline feature but not demonstrated in the showcase.
- Users need to see scrollback preserved with UI chrome stable.

Goals
- Create a screen that simulates a real CLI tool with streaming logs.
- Render a stable UI bar at top/bottom while logs scroll naturally.
- Show explicit "scrollback preserved" callout and mode indicator.

Menu/IA Fit
- Category: Core.
- Short label: "Inline".
- Screen Registry tags: "inline", "scrollback", "chrome".

Implementation Notes
- Use inline mode configuration and a deterministic log stream generator.
- Provide toggles for UI height, anchor (top/bottom), and log rate.
- Include an inline/alt-screen comparison toggle to show differences.
- Add a "scrollback test" action to append long logs quickly.

Acceptance Criteria
- Logs scroll without corrupting the UI region.
- Mode indicator reflects inline vs alt-screen status.
- UI height changes do not flicker or smear.
- Inline/alt comparison is obvious within 10 seconds of use.
- Screen registered in Screen Registry (category, label, tags, blurb).

Tests / Validation
- Snapshot tests at 80x24 and 120x40 with different UI heights/anchors.
- Unit tests for log generator determinism and viewport bounds.
- E2E PTY test in inline mode (extend `scripts/e2e_demo_showcase.sh`) with JSONL logs:
  - run_id, ui_height, anchor, seed, size, log_rate, outcome","notes":"Completed: Inline Mode Story screen + wiring; snapshots generated (inline_mode_story_*). Tests: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings. Full BLESS run was interrupted due to long proptest run; targeted BLESS for inline_mode_story_* succeeded.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:50:17.778674505Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T04:35:13.712625033Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T04:35:13.712606999Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.3","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:50:17.778674505Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.4","title":"Demo: Explainability Cockpit (Evidence Ledger Overlay)","description":"Background
- Evidence ledgers exist but are not visualized in a unified way.
- A cockpit view makes the "galaxy-brain" decisions feel real.

Goals
- Display diff strategy, BOCPD resize regime, and budget decisions in one panel.
- Show a short "why" line and key posterior values.
- Provide a compact timeline of recent decisions.

Menu/IA Fit
- Category: Systems.
- Short label: "Explain".
- Registry tags: "evidence", "bayes", "bocpd", "budget".

Implementation Notes
- Consume evidence ledgers from runtime/render and map to UI rows.
- Add a compact overlay mode to view alongside any screen.
- Deterministic when evidence sources are deterministic.

Acceptance Criteria
- Cockpit displays all three policy streams (diff, resize, budget).
- Each entry includes timestamp/frame, decision, and evidence summary.
- Overlay can be toggled without impacting underlying screen state.
- Empty-state UI is informative and non-noisy.
- Screen registered in Screen Registry (category, label, tags, blurb).

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for ledger-to-UI mapping and empty-state behavior.
- Snapshot tests for empty and populated states.
- E2E PTY run (extend `scripts/e2e_demo_showcase.sh`) with JSONL logs:
  - run_id, decision_type, frame_idx, evidence_summary_hash, outcome","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:50:24.838825596Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T03:05:15.976356788Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.27","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:51:59.673851066Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:52:03.318964853Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.4","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:50:24.838825596Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
